content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 7, 1990


James R. Felton
Burke, Williams and Sorensen
One Wilshire Building
624 South Grand Avenue, 11th Floor
Los Angeles, CA  90017

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-052

Dear Mr. Felton:

     This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

     Under the terms of its franchise agreement with the city, the
public access television channel will televise the meetings of the
city council.  Does inclusion of a photograph of the city council,
accompanied by the names of the councilmembers, in the program
guide violate the mass mailing provisions of the Act?

CONCLUSION

     Inclusion of a photograph of the city council, accompanied by
the names of the councilmembers, in the program guide would
violate the mass mailing provisions of the Act.  However, a roster
listing of the names of the councilmembers may be included in the
program guide.

FACTS

     The City of West Covina has entered into an agreement with
Mt. San Antonio College and Cencom Cable Television (Cencom).
This agreement has created an entity entitled MSAC Cablevsion
(MSAC).  Cencom has a nonexclusive franchise for the operation and
promotion of cable television within the city's boundaries.  MSAC
provides programming on a community access channel seen over


_________________________
¹  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-052
Page 2

Cencom pursuant to the franchise agreement. The city contributes
40 percent of MSAC's yearly budget.

The agreement also has created a cable communications co-
operative board, a public access subcommittee, and an education
access subcommittee. One member of the city staff sits on the
cable communications cooperative board which acts as an advisory
board to MSAC. No members of the city staff participate in the
two subcommittees.

Cencom is the only cable franchise within the city's
boundaries. Any resident of West Covina who wishes to obtain
cable television service must subscribe to Cencom. All solicita-
tion for new subscribers is done by Cencom. Subscribers receive
the Cencom program guide which includes programming on the public
access channel.

All of the employees of MSAC are employees of the college.
These employees are solely responsible for the printing of MSAC's
program guide which promotes programming on the community access
channel only. The city does not participate in the preparation of
MSAC's program guide and has no control over its contents. All of
MSAC's bulk mail is run through a bulk mail permit of the college.
MSAC distributes its program guide to all subscribers to Cencom.

Under the franchise agreement, Cencom will televise the meet-
ings of the West Covina City Council on the public access channel.
MSAC wants to advertise in its program guide that the meetings
will be televised to the residents of West Covina. The program
guide will be distributed to more than 200 subscribers. MSAC has
asked the permission of the city council to include a photograph
of the city council in its program guide. This photograph will be
used for advertising purposes and to notify the residents of West
Covina that the meetings of the city council will be televised.
The councilmembers' names would be printed along with the
photograph.

You are requesting advice on behalf of the City of West
Covina to determine whether the use of a photograph of the city
council in the program guide would violate the mass mailing provi-
sions of the Act. You also want to determine whether the use of
the councilmembers' names alone would be similarly violative of
the provisions of the Act. You are especially interested in the
applicability of our Harper Advice Letter, No. 1-89-45 (copy
enclosed), to your facts. In Harper, the staff found that the
inclusion of a photograph of elected school board members in
school yearbooks would not violate the mass mailing provisions of
the Act. You believe that your facts are sufficiently analogous
to the facts in Harper, supra, to warrant the conclusion that
inclusion of a photograph of the city council with the names of
the councilmembers in the program guide would not violate the
provisions of the Act.

File No. A-90-052
Page 3

ANALYSIS

The Act defines a mass mailing as two hundred or more
substantially similar pieces of mail. (Section 82041.5.) Section
89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be
sent at public expense. Read together and taken literally, these
two sections could be construed to ban all mailings of 200 or more
substantially similar documents if the mailings are done at public
expense.

The Commission has enacted Regulation 189012 (copy enclosed)
to implement the provisions of Section 89001. Under Regulation
18901, a mass mailing is prohibited if it "features" an elected
officer "affiliated" with the agency which produces or sends the
mailing, or includes the name, office, photograph, or other refer-
ence to an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing and the mailing is "prepared or sent
in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer." (Regulation 18901(a)(2).) A mass mailing is
also prohibited if any of the costs of distribution is paid for
with public moneys or costs of design, production, and printing
exceeding $50.00 are paid with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the intent of sending the
item. (Regulation 18901(a)(3).) For purposes of our discussion,
we assume that preparation of a photograph of the city council
would cost in excess of $50.

An elected official is "affiliated" with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing if the official is a member, of-
ficer, or employee of the agency, or of a subunit thereof, such as
a committee, or who has supervisory control over the agency, or
who appoints one or more members of the agency. (Regulation
18901(c)(1).) We conclude that the city council is affiliated
with MSAC because the city council has supervisory control over
MSAC through the cable communication cooperative board. This
board is, in fact, an advisory board which controls the activities
of MSAC. Moreover, the city council appoints one member of this
board and contributes 40 percent of the operating budget of MSAC.
It is clear, then, that the city council is affiliated with MSAC.

However, affiliation alone does not make the use of an
elected official's name in a mass mailing prohibited. The second
prong of the test is whether the mailing is prepared or sent in

_______________________

2 The Commission approved the repeal and reenactment of Regula-
tion 18901 in December 1989. The regulation approved by the Com-
mission will be submitted to the Office of Administrative Law in
the near future. Although the new regulation is not in effect
until reviewed by the Office of Administrative Law and filed with
the Secretary of State, the new regulation represents the current
policy of the Commission and we are advising you accordingly.

File No. A-90-052
Page 4

cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the
elected officer. Where the mailing has been prepared in
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the
elected officer, any use of the elected officer's name, photograph
or office, or any reference to the officer is prohibited.
(Regulation 18901((a)(2)(B).)

If the mailing has not been prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer,
use of the elected officer's name is permitted provided the official is not featured in the mailing. (Regulation
18901((a)(2)(A).) A publication "features" an elected officer if
the item mailed includes the elected officer's photograph or
signature, or singles out the elected officer by the manner of
display of his or her name or office in the layout of the
document, such as by headlines, captions, type size, typeface, or
type color. (Regulation 18901(c)(2).)

Publication of pictures of the city council in the MSAC
program guide would "feature" the public officials. Because your
facts indicate that the city council is affiliated with MSAC,
which will produce the program guide, inclusion of a photograph of
the city council in the program guide is prohibited. However,
publication of a roster listing of the name and title of each
councilmember is permissible provided that the names of all the
elected officers appear in the same type size, typeface, type
color, and location on the page. (Regulation 18901(b)(1).)

We reached a different conclusion in Harper, supra, because
purchase of the yearbooks is an "unsolicited" request. A request
is unsolicited when it is not induced by the elected officer or
any third person acting at his or her behest. (Regulation
18901(c)(4)(A).) School board members play no role in inducing
students to purchase the yearbooks. If any solicitation occurs,
it is on behalf of other students involved in yearbook production,
not on behalf of board members. Thus, the yearbooks are
"unsolicited" and are not "mass mailings" subject to the restrictions of the Act.

Conversely, the MSAC program guides are sent to all those who
subscribe to Cencom. Subscribers to Cencom expect to receive the
Cencom program guide. Receipt of the Cencom program guide is part
and parcel of their contract for cable television. Regulation
18901(c)(4)(E) provides as follows:

A person who subscribes to newspapers or other
periodicals published by persons other than elected
officers shall be deemed to have made unsolicited
requests for material published in those subscription publications.

The Commission has advised that this subdivision includes
periodicals to which a person subscribes as well as periodicals
published by membership organizations to which the member belongs.

File No. A-90-052
Page 5

Thus, persons who subscribe to Cencom will be deemed to have made an unsolicited request to receive Cencom's program guide, including a picture of the city council which might be published therein. However, they will not be deemed to have made an unsolicited request to receive materials from third parties who are using Cencom's mailing list. The public access program guides distributed by MSAC constitute such third-party mailings. Therefore, material published in the MSAC guide does not fall within the exception set forth in subdivision (c)(4)(E).

Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosures
